sentences.target: J'avoue mon ignorance sur ce sujet.

translations.fr: J'avoue mon ignorance sur ce sujet.
translations.en: I admit my ignorance about this topic.
translations.es: Reconozco mi ignorancia sobre este tema.
translations.de: Ich gestehe meine Unwissenheit zu diesem Thema ein.
translations.it: Ammetto la mia ignoranza su questo argomento.
translations.ko: 이 주제에 대해 제 무지를 인정합니다.
translations.zh-Hans: 我承认自己对这个话题知之甚少。
translations.yue: 我承認自己對呢個話題所知不多。
translations.ru: Я признаю свою неосведомлённость по этой теме.
translations.pt: Eu admito minha ignorância sobre este assunto.
translations.vi: Tôi thừa nhận sự thiếu hiểu biết của mình về chủ đề này.
translations.nl: Ik erken mijn onwetendheid over dit onderwerp.
translations.uk: Я визнаю свою необізнаність щодо цієї теми.
translations.hu: Beismerem a tudatlanságomat ebben a témában.
translations.hi: मैं इस विषय के बारे में अपनी अज्ञानता स्वीकार करता हूँ.
translations.pl: Przyznaję się do swojej ignorancji w tym temacie.
translations.el: Ομολογώ την άγνοιά μου για αυτό το θέμα.
translations.nb: Jeg innrømmer min uvitenhet om dette emnet.
translations.id: Saya mengakui ketidaktahuan saya tentang topik ini.
translations.sv: Jag medger min okunskap i detta ämne.
translations.ro: Îmi recunosc ignoranța în legătură cu acest subiect.
translations.cs: Přiznávám svou nevědomost v této věci.
translations.da: Jeg indrømmer min uvidenhed om dette emne.
translations.ja: この話題について自分の無知を認めます。

tokens.explanation: 1st person singular present indicative with elided clitic subject pronoun (j' = je) attached.
1st person singular possessive determiner; used before a vowel-initial feminine noun ('ignorance').
Common noun, feminine singular; head of the NP 'mon ignorance', direct object of 'J'avoue'.
Preposition introducing the prepositional complement 'ce sujet'.
Demonstrative determiner, masculine singular, modifying 'sujet'.
Common noun, masculine singular; object of the preposition 'sur'.

translations.es: A veces tu ignorancia me cansa.
translations.it: La tua ignoranza a volte mi stanca.
translations.ko: 네 무지는 가끔 저를 지치게 합니다.
translations.zh-Hans: 有时你的无知让我很累。
translations.yue: 有時你嘅無知令我好攰。
translations.pt: Às vezes sua ignorância me cansa.
translations.vi: Sự thiếu hiểu biết của bạn đôi khi làm tôi mệt mỏi.
translations.nl: Jouw onwetendheid maakt me soms moe.
translations.hi: तुम्हारी अज्ञानता कभी-कभी मुझे थका देती है.
translations.el: Η άγνοιά σου με κουράζει μερικές φορές.
translations.nb: Din uvitenhet gjør meg noen ganger sliten.
translations.sv: Din okunskap gör mig ibland trött.
translations.da: Din uvidenhed gør mig nogle gange træt.
translations.ja: あなたの無知は時々私を疲れさせます。

tokens.explanation: 2nd person singular possessive determiner; form used before a vowel-initial feminine noun ('ignorance').
Common noun, feminine singular; subject of 'fatigue' with determiner 'Ton'.
1st person singular weak object pronoun; direct object of 'fatigue'.
3rd person singular present indicative; agrees with subject 'Ton ignorance'.
Adverb of frequency modifying the verb phrase.

sentences.target: On paie pour son ignorance plus tard.
sentences.reading: /ɔ̃ pɛ puʁ sɔ̃ iɲɔʁɑ̃s ply taʁ/

translations.fr: On paie pour son ignorance plus tard.
translations.ipa: /ɔ̃ pɛ puʁ sɔ̃ iɲɔʁɑ̃s ply taʁ/
translations.en: You pay for your ignorance later.
translations.es: La ignorancia se paga más tarde.
translations.de: Für die eigene Unwissenheit zahlt man später.
translations.it: Si paga la propria ignoranza più tardi.
translations.zh-Hans: 以后要为无知付出代价。
translations.yue: 遲啲要為無知付出代價。
translations.vi: Sau này phải trả giá cho sự thiếu hiểu biết.
translations.nl: Voor je onwetendheid betaal je later.
translations.hu: A tudatlanságért később fizet az ember.
translations.hi: अज्ञानता की कीमत बाद में चुकानी पड़ती है.
translations.sv: Man får betala för okunskap senare.
translations.ja: 無知の代償は後で払うことになる。

tokens.explanation: Impersonal/inclusive subject pronoun; triggers 3rd person singular agreement.
3rd person singular present indicative; main predicate with subject 'On'.
Preposition introducing the complement 'son ignorance' (cause/purpose).
3rd person singular possessive determiner; used before vowel-initial feminine noun ('ignorance').
Common noun, feminine singular; object of the preposition 'pour'.
Adverb; with 'tard' forms the temporal adverbial 'plus tard' = 'later'.
Adverb; in the expression 'plus tard' indicating a later time.

sentences.target: Nous sortons de l'ignorance petit à petit.
sentences.reading: /nu sɔʁtɔ̃ də liɲɔʁɑ̃s pəti a pəti/

translations.fr: Nous sortons de l'ignorance petit à petit.
translations.ipa: /nu sɔʁtɔ̃ də liɲɔʁɑ̃s pəti a pəti/
translations.en: We are getting out of ignorance little by little.
translations.es: Vamos saliendo de la ignorancia poco a poco.
translations.de: Wir kommen nach und nach aus der Unwissenheit heraus.
translations.it: Stiamo uscendo dall'ignoranza poco a poco.
translations.pt: Estamos saindo da ignorância aos poucos.
translations.vi: Chúng tôi đang thoát khỏi sự thiếu hiểu biết từng chút một.
translations.ar: نحن نخرج من الجهل شيئًا فشيئًا.
translations.nl: We komen stap voor stap uit de onwetendheid.
translations.hi: हम धीरे-धीरे अज्ञानता से बाहर निकल रहे हैं.
translations.el: Βγαίνουμε από την άγνοια σιγά σιγά.
translations.nb: Vi kommer sakte ut av uvitenhet.
translations.sv: Vi tar oss steg för steg ur okunskap.
translations.da: Vi kommer lidt efter lidt ud af uvidenhed.
translations.ja: 私たちは少しずつ無知から抜け出している。

tokens.explanation: 1st person plural subject pronoun.
1st person plural present indicative; main verb of the clause.
Preposition introducing the complement 'l'ignorance'.
Common noun with elided definite article (l'); object of the preposition 'de'.
Adjective used adverbially in the fixed expression 'petit à petit' (meaning 'gradually').
Preposition linking the parts of the fixed adverbial expression 'petit à petit'.
Second occurrence completing the fixed adverbial 'petit à petit' = 'little by little'.

On paie pour son ignorance plus tard.
Nous sortons de l'ignorance petit à petit.

You pay for your ignorance later.
We are getting out of ignorance little by little.
